extraction.one_line_summary: ROJAS Jose won TTT with Movistar setting fastest time, though team was later penalized for pushing incident discovered in Stage 3 footage
tactical_patterns: Movistar unexpectedly dominated TTT despite not being noted for discipline, revealing underestimated collective strength in 2017 roster
BMC fielded dual leadership with Porte and Van Garderen, splitting resources rather than consolidating behind single GC threat
Trek/Contador lost 1:15 in TTT to top teams, immediately compromising GC despite individual strength—team weakness exposed early

rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: Movistar rallied around him as sole leader after Quintana's absence, benefiting from undivided team focus in TTT
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: negative: Trek team 1:15 slower than top TTT squads, revealing chronic collective weakness that would handicap any GC bid
rider_signals.VAN GARDEREN Tejay: neutral: BMC split focus between him and Porte rather than committing to hierarchy, diluting tactical clarity
